Caption: POV: You said "walk" 20 minutes ago and your dog hasn't forgotten 👁️👄👁️

Expression: intense, unblinking, slightly judgmental. Natural setting (living room, kitchen, or doorway). Meme-style framing (can add simple text overlay if needed: "It's been 20 minutes, Karen")

Caption:
From tiny paws to legendary status 🐾✨

Show us your pup's glow-up! Drop a before/after in the comments (or tag us in your post) and let's celebrate how far your small legend has come.

JOCK Puppy — nutrition that grows with them (2-12 months, small to medium breeds).
Visual_Brief: Split image (left/right or top/bottom): Left - Tiny puppy (8-10 weeks) small, adorable, ears too big for head. Right - Same breed, 10-12 months bigger, confident, still playful but more grown. Breed: Medium dog (Staffy, Border Collie, or Beagle ideal). Natural setting, lifestyle feel (not studio)

Caption: The best part of the day isn't the adventure. It's who you share it with 💚
Visual_Brief: Quiet moment between dog and owner — eye contact, head resting on lap, sitting together, or walking side-by-side. Medium to large breed (any from JOCK range). Natural setting (home, garden, or outdoor). Emotion: calm, content, connected (not high-energy). Golden hour light or soft natural light preferred. Authentic, candid feel (not staged). No product visible — this is pure brand emotion

Caption:
Legends aren't born. They're trained 💪🦴

Whether you're teaching "sit", mastering recall, or just rewarding good behaviour — Happy Happies Biscuits are your secret weapon. Biltong, Boerewors, or Braaivleis flavour (because even training treats should taste like home).

Small and large biscuits in every pack. Suitable for all breeds.

What's the best trick your dog knows? Drop it in the comments 👇
Visual_Brief: Reel Storyboard: 0-3s Quick cuts of dog failing/fumbling a trick (playful, not frustrated). 3-5s Owner holds up Happy Happies biscuit — dog's attention locks in. 5-8s Dog nails the trick (sit, paw, spin, or stay). 8-10s Reward moment — happy crunch, tail wag, proud dog. 10-12s Product pack reveal with "Legends in Training" text overlay. Dog: Medium to large breed (Labrador, Golden Retriever, or Ridgeback ideal). Setting: Natural outdoor (garden or training area). Vibe: Fun, relatable, satisfying payoff

Caption:

Let's see who the real dog experts are 👇

Slide 2 - Border Collie (distinctive markings/eye color). Slide 3 - Golden Retriever (partial face or paw). Slide 4 - Boerboel (powerful build, distinctive head shape). Caption:
Weekend mode: activated 🐕💨

Active dogs need fuel that keeps up with them. JOCK Multistage is built for dogs who don't do "chill" — whether it's chasing tennis balls, exploring the veld, or just being gloriously chaotic.

All-in-one nutrition for adult dogs who live life at full speed.

What does your dog's perfect Saturday look like? 👇
Visual_Brief: Medium to large breed dog (Labrador, Golden Retriever, Border Collie, or Ridgeback) mid-action — running, jumping, or playing. Natural outdoor setting (garden, field, or open space). Dog looks energetic, happy, fully engaged. Product bag visible in background or naturally placed nearby. Action shot preferred (not posed/static). Natural daylight, candid feel

Caption:
No thoughts. Just chaos 💨🐕

That 8pm energy hits different.
Visual_Brief: Dog mid-zoomies — motion blur, mid-run, tail up, ears back, pure chaos energy. Medium to large breed (any from JOCK range). Natural outdoor setting (garden or yard preferred). Blurred background to emphasize speed. Expression: pure unhinged joy. Can add simple text overlay: "Brain: empty. Speed: maximum."

Caption:
A little grey never hurt anyone. Still legendary 💚

Senior dogs aren't slowing down — they're just getting wiser. JOCK Senior supports joints, digestion, and everything that keeps your old-timer thriving (7+ years).

Because legends deserve respect at every age.

Drop a pic of your senior legend in the comments 👇
Visual_Brief: Senior dog (7+ years) with visible grey fur — Boerboel or German Shepherd ideal (move away from default Lab/Golden/Ridgeback). Grey muzzle shows dramatically on a Boerboel's dark or brindle coat. Dog looking healthy, alert, dignified — not lying down or sleepy. SA outdoor setting: farm stoep, veld, or suburban garden with mature trees. Expression: calm, wise, still fully engaged. Product bag visible naturally in frame. Warm golden hour light preferred.

Caption:
POV: He's 42kg and still thinks your lap is his 🐾😂

Tag someone with a giant who never got the memo about personal space — drop your best photos in the comments, we want to see SA's most dramatic large dogs 👇
Visual_Brief: Rottweiler, Great Dane, or Boerboel (8-18 months) in full "teenager" mode — draped across owner's lap, sitting on owner's feet, or wedged into a spot clearly too small for them. Expression: completely unbothered, zero awareness of size. Natural SA home setting — couch, stoep, or living room tiled floor. No product in frame. Authentic, warm, funny — dog looks healthy and content, not distressed. Avoid the default Lab/Golden here; the comedy works best with a genuinely giant breed.

Caption:
Public holiday = quality time with your legend 🐾🇿🇦

Happy Human Rights Day, South Africa. However you're spending it — beach walk, backyard braai, or just a lazy morning on the stoep — make it count.

What are you and your dog getting up to today?
Visual_Brief: Africanis or South African mixed breed dog and owner spending quality time together — stoep morning, community park, township yard, or garden. SA setting should feel real and inclusive, not aspirational-suburban. Dog is relaxed, happy, fully connected to owner. No product visible. Warm, authentic, diverse. Human Rights Day is the right moment for a genuinely representative SA dog — not a pedigree in a manicured garden. Soft natural light or morning golden hour.
